_meta.description: Curated plumbing and HVAC vocabulary for inbound AI agent training. Maps customer lay language to technical terms, organized by fixture/system.

under_sink_supply.customer: the curved pipe under the drain
the first piece of pipe from the drain
the flexible tubes connecting to the faucet
the thing that screws on the drain hole
the pipe going into the wall under the sink
the screen at the tip of the faucet
the part inside the faucet that controls water
the little chrome cap on the sink near the faucet

toilet.technical: faulty flapper or fill valve — water bypassing flapper seat

toilet.customer: the flushing part / the handle arm
the rubber thing at the bottom of the tank

toilet.technical: float ball (older) or float cup (modern fill valve)

toilet.customer: the valve that fills the tank
the big opening at the bottom of the tank
the wax seal / the ring at the bottom of the toilet
the bolts that hold the toilet down
the rubber gasket between tank and bowl
the supply tube at the wall behind the toilet
the cap at the base of the toilet
the tall tube in the middle of the tank
toilet.technical: overflow tube (prevents flood if fill valve fails)

toilet.customer: the small tube that goes inside the overflow tube
the drain fitting in the floor

bathtub.customer: the knob that switches between tub and shower
the drain stopper that won't open or close
bathtub.technical: trip lever drain assembly / pop-up stopper

bathtub.customer: the cover plate thing on the tub with the lever
bathtub.technical: trip lever face plate / overflow plate

bathtub.customer: the decorative trim plate around the valve handles
the piece under the tub connecting to the drain
the pipe inside the wall behind the overflow plate

shower.customer: the cartridge that keeps the temperature from changing
the pipe coming out of the wall to the showerhead
the waterproof membrane under the tile floor
the step at the shower entry

water_heater.customer: the pipe thing on the side with a lever
water_heater.technical: T&P valve (temperature and pressure relief valve)

water_heater.customer: the overflow pipe / the dripping pipe on the side
the pipe on top of the water heater
water_heater.technical: cold water inlet / hot water outlet / dip tube depending on position

water_heater.customer: the small extra tank near the water heater
the valve at the top where water comes in
the inside rod that prevents tank corrosion

water_heater.technical: dip tube (sends cold water to bottom of tank for heating)

sewer_main_line.customer: the cap in the floor / the cap in the yard

hvac_general.customer: the outside unit / the box outside / the compressor unit
the coil thing above the furnace / the A-coil
the white PVC pipe dripping outside
the thing in the attic / the box in the attic
the cylinder can inside the outside unit
the electrical switch for the compressor
the copper pipes between inside and outside
the drain pan under the indoor coil
the safety switch that shuts off AC if drain clogs

furnace.customer: the glowing element that lights the gas
the small rod that detects the flame
the motor that exhausts combustion gases
the part that transfers heat (if cracked, dangerous)
the valve that controls gas flow to burners
the circuit board / the brain of the furnace
the safety switch that shuts off if furnace overheats
the white pipe dripping from furnace

heat_pump.customer: the valve that switches between heating and cooling modes

heat_pump.technical: emergency heat mode (backup mode using only electric strips)

heat_pump.customer: steam coming off the outdoor unit in winter
heat_pump.technical: normal defrost cycle — NOT an emergency

ductwork.customer: the large box attached to the furnace
the thing that blocks air in the duct

mini_split.customer: the unit mounted high on the wall
mini_split.technical: indoor air handler / wall-mount head unit

mini_split.customer: the pump that drains the indoor unit
the copper pipes + wire running between indoor and outdoor

washing_machine_laundry.customer: the two knobs behind the washer
the hoses from the wall to the washer
the pipe the washer drain hose goes into

dishwasher.customer: the chrome device on the sink deck
the hose that drains into the disposal
the shutoff under the sink for the dishwasher

sounds.technical_cause: water hammer — pressure surge from sudden valve closure
worn valves with deposit buildup or high pressure

sounds.customer_description: ticking / tapping / sputtering in the pipes
popping / rumbling from the water heater
sounds.technical_cause: sediment buildup (scale at bottom of tank)
fill valve seat wear or flapper bypass

sounds.customer_description: clicking from the furnace that won't start
sounds.technical_cause: faulty igniter or gas valve; flame sensor misread

sounds.customer_description: banging when the furnace or AC turns on
sounds.technical_cause: duct thermal expansion (oil-canning) or undersized ductwork

sounds.customer_description: loud rumbling from the outdoor unit

sounds.technical_cause: delayed ignition from gas buildup — urgent

sight.technical_cause: sediment, depleted anode rod, or corroded iron pipes

sight.customer_description: ice on the pipes or outside AC unit
sight.technical_cause: low refrigerant or airflow restriction (dirty filter or failed blower)

sight.customer_description: water pooling under the water heater
sight.technical_cause: T&P valve relief, tank corrosion, or failed drain valve

sight.customer_description: steam coming off the outdoor unit in winter
sight.technical_cause: NORMAL heat pump defrost cycle — not an emergency

sight.customer_description: yellow or sooty flame on furnace
sight.technical_cause: combustion problem; dirty burners or gas pressure issue — urgent

smell.technical_cause: gas leak (mercaptan odorant) — EMERGENCY: evacuate and call 911
dry P-trap, broken wax ring, or vent pipe issue

smell.customer_description: burning smell from furnace at first use of season
smell.technical_cause: dust burnoff (usually benign) — escalate if persistent or accompanied by smoke
mold in ductwork or on evaporator coil
EMERGENCY — potential gas leak or cracked heat exchanger

touch_feel.technical_cause: subfloor rot from wax ring failure — elevated urgency

touch_feel.customer_description: warm air from vents when AC is on
weak water pressure only at one faucet
touch_feel.technical_cause: aerator clog or shut-off valve partially closed

common_misidentifications.what_customer_thinks_it_is: the overflow pipe on the side of the water heater
common_misidentifications.training_note: If customer says 'dripping from the pipe on the side', ask if there's a lever on it

common_misidentifications.what_customer_thinks_it_is: a leak from a small extra tank
common_misidentifications.what_it_actually_is: expansion tank — normal component, may indicate T&P valve issue if wet

common_misidentifications.what_customer_thinks_it_is: steam / smoke from the outdoor AC unit in winter
common_misidentifications.what_it_actually_is: normal heat pump defrost cycle — customer should NOT be dispatched an emergency tech
condenser unit (the compressor is one component inside the condenser)
return air plenum / return grille housing

common_complaints.customer_says: water under the sink
disposal hums but doesn't grind
likely_causes: jammed impeller — check reset button under unit

common_complaints.customer_says: disposal leaking from the bottom
likely_causes: failed internal seal — unit must be replaced

common_complaints.customer_says: disposal won't turn on at all
faucet drips when off
low pressure only at kitchen faucet
dishwasher water backs up into sink
the stopper won't stay up
stopper stuck down / won't come up
tub won't drain
likely_causes: trip lever stopper stuck in closed position

common_complaints.customer_says: the lever thing doesn't work
likely_causes: trip lever mechanism failure or adjustment needed

common_complaints.customer_says: water comes out spout and showerhead at same time
can't switch from tub to shower
likely_causes: diverter stuck due to mineral buildup

common_complaints.customer_says: slow drip from the spout
temperature fluctuates when toilet flushes
can't get water hot or cold enough
likely_causes: cartridge reach limiter or anti-scald set too low

common_complaints.customer_says: leak from behind the shower wall
toilet keeps running
toilet runs for a few seconds then stops (ghost flushing)
likely_causes: slow flapper leak — water slowly draining through worn flapper

common_complaints.customer_says: toilet won't flush / handle does nothing
water on floor at the toilet base
toilet rocks
tank won't fill
toilet flushes slowly or weak flush
toilet gurgles when not flushing
washing machine standpipe overflows
hose leaking at the washer connection
water on floor during wash cycle
washing machine won't drain

laundry.notes: Proper standpipe height: 18–24 inches minimum, 96 inches maximum (prevents siphoning)

common_complaints.customer_says: dishwasher has standing water after cycle
dishwasher leaks under sink
water backing up into sink when dishwasher drains
no hot water — gas unit
no hot water — electric unit
not enough hot water / runs out fast
popping / rumbling / banging from water heater
rusty or discolored hot water
water heater leaking from the bottom
T&P valve dripping or discharging
pilot light won't stay lit
sulfur or rotten egg smell in hot water
likely_causes: anode rod reacting with high-sulfur water

common_complaints.customer_says: multiple drains are slow or backing up
likely_causes: main line involvement — NOT individual clogs
common_complaints.escalation: reclassify job type to main line

common_complaints.customer_says: when I flush the toilet the sink gurgles
water comes up in the bathtub when I run the dishwasher
sewage smell throughout the house
likely_causes: dry trap OR main line/vent issue

common_complaints.customer_says: sewage coming up through the floor drain in the basement
AC running but not cooling
outside unit not running but inside is
ice on the pipes or unit freezing up
water dripping or flooding from inside unit
not reaching temperature
furnace won't turn on / nothing happens
furnace starts then shuts off immediately
furnace short cycles
furnace blowing cold air
clicking from furnace that won't start
likely_causes: igniter firing but gas not reaching

common_complaints.customer_says: loud bang when furnace starts
likely_causes: delayed ignition from gas buildup — urgent

common_complaints.customer_says: gas smell from furnace area
likely_causes: gas leak or cracked heat exchanger — EMERGENCY

common_complaints.customer_says: heat pump blowing cold air in heat mode
thick ice covering the entire outdoor unit
steam coming off the outdoor unit in winter
likely_causes: NORMAL defrost cycle — do not dispatch emergency tech

common_complaints.customer_says: heat pump not switching from heat to cool
indoor unit dripping water inside
one room not cooling but others are fine
unit displays error code

location_descriptions.under the kitchen sink: beneath kitchen sink cabinet — typically P-trap, supply lines, angle stops, disposal
location_descriptions.the pipe behind the toilet: toilet supply line or shut-off valve
location_descriptions.under my bathroom sink: P-trap area, pop-up assembly, supply lines
location_descriptions.in the wall: in-wall plumbing — typically shower valve body
location_descriptions.in my crawlspace: sub-floor drain lines or supply runs
location_descriptions.the pipes in my attic: HVAC condensate drain or supply/drain stack
location_descriptions.the cap in the yard: clean-out access port for main sewer line
